1→/*
  2→ * Copyright 2019-2023 JetBrains s.r.o. and contributors.
  3→ * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4→ */
  5→
  6→package kotlinx.datetime.internal.format
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.DecimalFraction
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.formatter.*
 10→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.*
 11→
 12→/**
 13→ * A directive that specifies a way to parse and format the [field].
 14→ */
 15→internal interface FieldFormatDirective<in Target> {
 16→    /**
 17→     * The field parsed and formatted by this directive.
 18→     */
 19→    val field: FieldSpec<Target, *>
 20→
 21→    /**
 22→     * The formatter operation that formats the field.
 23→     */
 24→    fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target>
 25→
 26→    /**
 27→     * The parser structure that parses the field.
 28→     */
 29→    fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target>
 30→
 31→    /**
 32→     * The string with the code that, when evaluated in the builder context, appends the directive.
 33→     */
 34→    val builderRepresentation: String
 35→}
 36→
 37→/**
 38→ * A directive for a decimal format of an integer field that is known to be unsigned.
 39→ * The field is formatted with the field padded to [minDigits] with zeroes,
 40→ * and the parser expects the field to be at least [minDigits] digits long.
 41→ */
 42→internal abstract class UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
 43→    final override val field: UnsignedFieldSpec<Target>,
 44→    private val minDigits: Int,
 45→    private val spacePadding: Int?,
 46→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
 47→
 48→    private val maxDigits: Int = field.maxDigits
 49→
 50→    init {
 51→        require(minDigits >= 0) {
 52→            "The minimum number of digits ($minDigits) is negative"
 53→        }
 54→        require(maxDigits >= minDigits) {
 55→            "The maximum number of digits ($maxDigits) is less than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
 56→        }
 57→        if (spacePadding != null) {
 58→            require(spacePadding > minDigits) {
 59→                "The space padding ($spacePadding) should be more than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
 60→            }
 61→        }
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> {
 65→        val formatter = UnsignedIntFormatterStructure(
 66→            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
 67→            zeroPadding = minDigits,
 68→        )
 69→        return if (spacePadding != null) SpacePaddedFormatter(formatter, spacePadding) else formatter
 70→    }
 71→
 72→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
 73→        spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, maxDigits, spacePadding, field.accessor, field.name)
 74→}
 75→
 76→/**
 77→ * A directive for a string-based format of an integer field that is known to be unsigned.
 78→ */
 79→internal abstract class NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
 80→    final override val field: UnsignedFieldSpec<Target>,
 81→    private val values: List<String>,
 82→    private val name: String,
 83→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
 84→
 85→    init {
 86→        require(values.size == field.maxValue - field.minValue + 1) {
 87→            "The number of values (${values.size}) in $values does not match the range of the field (${field.maxValue - field.minValue + 1})"
 88→        }
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    private fun getStringValue(target: Target): String = field.accessor.getterNotNull(target).let {
 92→        values.getOrNull(it-field.minValue)
 93→            ?: "The value $it of ${field.name} does not have a corresponding string representation"
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    private inner class AssignableString: AssignableField<Target, String> {
 97→        override fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Target, newValue: String): String? =
 98→            field.accessor.trySetWithoutReassigning(container, values.indexOf(newValue) + field.minValue)?.let {
 99→                values[it - field.minValue]
100→            }
101→
102→        override val name: String get() = this@NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective.name
103→    }
104→
105→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
106→        StringFormatterStructure(::getStringValue)
107→
108→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
109→        ParserStructure(
110→            listOf(
111→                StringSetParserOperation(values, AssignableString(), "One of $values for $name")
112→            ), emptyList()
113→        )
114→}
115→
116→/**
117→ * A directive for a string-based format of an enum field.
118→ */
119→internal abstract class NamedEnumIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target, Type>(
120→    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Type>,
121→    private val mapping: Map<Type, String>,
122→    private val name: String,
123→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
124→
125→    private val reverseMapping = mapping.entries.associate { it.value to it.key }
126→
127→    private fun getStringValue(target: Target): String = field.accessor.getterNotNull(target).let {
128→        mapping[field.accessor.getterNotNull(target)]
129→            ?: "The value $it of ${field.name} does not have a corresponding string representation"
130→    }
131→
132→    private inner class AssignableString: AssignableField<Target, String> {
133→        override fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Target, newValue: String): String? =
134→            field.accessor.trySetWithoutReassigning(container, reverseMapping[newValue]!!)?.let { mapping[it] }
135→
136→        override val name: String get() = this@NamedEnumIntFieldFormatDirective.name
137→    }
138→
139→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
140→        StringFormatterStructure(::getStringValue)
141→
142→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
143→        ParserStructure(
144→            listOf(
145→                StringSetParserOperation(mapping.values, AssignableString(), "One of ${mapping.values} for $name")
146→            ), emptyList()
147→        )
148→}
149→
150→internal abstract class StringFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
151→    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, String>,
152→    private val acceptedStrings: Set<String>,
153→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
154→
155→    init {
156→        require(acceptedStrings.isNotEmpty()) {
157→            "The set of accepted strings is empty"
158→        }
159→    }
160→
161→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
162→        StringFormatterStructure(field.accessor::getterNotNull)
163→
164→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
165→        ParserStructure(
166→            listOf(StringSetParserOperation(acceptedStrings, field.accessor, field.name)),
167→            emptyList()
168→        )
169→}
170→
171→internal abstract class SignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
172→    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Int>,
173→    private val minDigits: Int?,
174→    private val maxDigits: Int?,
175→    private val spacePadding: Int?,
176→    private val outputPlusOnExceededWidth: Int?,
177→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
178→
179→    init {
180→        require(minDigits == null || minDigits >= 0) { "The minimum number of digits ($minDigits) is negative" }
181→        require(maxDigits == null || minDigits == null || maxDigits >= minDigits) {
182→            "The maximum number of digits ($maxDigits) is less than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
183→        }
184→    }
185→
186→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> {
187→        val formatter = SignedIntFormatterStructure(
188→            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
189→            zeroPadding = minDigits ?: 0,
190→            outputPlusOnExceededWidth = outputPlusOnExceededWidth,
191→        )
192→        return if (spacePadding != null) SpacePaddedFormatter(formatter, spacePadding) else formatter
193→    }
194→
195→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
196→        SignedIntParser(
197→            minDigits = minDigits,
198→            maxDigits = maxDigits,
199→            spacePadding = spacePadding,
200→            field.accessor,
201→            field.name,
202→            plusOnExceedsWidth = outputPlusOnExceededWidth,
203→        )
204→}
205→
206→internal abstract class DecimalFractionFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
207→    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, DecimalFraction>,
208→    private val minDigits: Int,
209→    private val maxDigits: Int,
210→    private val zerosToAdd: List<Int>,
211→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
212→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
213→        DecimalFractionFormatterStructure(field.accessor::getterNotNull, minDigits, maxDigits, zerosToAdd)
214→
215→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> = ParserStructure(
216→        listOf(
217→            NumberSpanParserOperation(
218→                listOf(FractionPartConsumer(minDigits, maxDigits, field.accessor, field.name))
219→            )
220→        ),
221→        emptyList()
222→    )
223→}
224→
225→internal abstract class ReducedIntFieldDirective<in Target>(
226→    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Int>,
227→    private val digits: Int,
228→    private val base: Int,
229→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
230→
231→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
232→        ReducedIntFormatterStructure(
233→            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
234→            digits = digits,
235→            base = base,
236→        )
237→
238→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
239→        ReducedIntParser(digits = digits, base = base, field.accessor, field.name)
240→}

 12→/**
 13→ * The date part of [LocalDateTime].
 14→ *
 15→ * This class represents dates without a reference to a particular time zone.
 16→ * As such, these objects may denote different spans of time in different time zones: for someone in Berlin,
 17→ * `2020-08-30` started and ended at different moments from those for someone in Tokyo.
 18→ *
 19→ * The arithmetic on [LocalDate] values is defined independently of the time zone (so `2020-08-30` plus one day
 20→ * is `2020-08-31` everywhere): see various [LocalDate.plus] and [LocalDate.minus] functions, as well
 21→ * as [LocalDate.periodUntil] and various other [*until][LocalDate.daysUntil] functions.
 22→ */
 23→@Serializable(with = LocalDateIso8601Serializer::class)
 24→public expect class LocalDate : Comparable<LocalDate> {
 25→    public companion object {
 26→        /**
 27→         * A shortcut for calling [DateTimeFormat.parse].
 28→         *
 29→         * Parses a string that represents a date and returns the parsed [LocalDate] value.
 30→         *
 31→         * If [format] is not specified, [Formats.ISO] is used.
 32→         *
 33→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [LocalDate] are exceeded.
 34→         *
 35→         * @see LocalDate.toString for formatting using the default format.
 36→         * @see LocalDate.format for formatting using a custom format.
 37→         */
 38→        public fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> = getIsoDateFormat()): LocalDate
 39→
 40→        /**
 41→         * Returns a [LocalDate] that is [epochDays] number of days from the epoch day `1970-01-01`.
 42→         *
 43→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the result exceeds the platform-specific boundaries of [LocalDate].
 44→         *
 45→         * @see LocalDate.toEpochDays
 46→         */
 47→        public fun fromEpochDays(epochDays: Int): LocalDate
 48→
 49→        /**
 50→         * Creates a new format for parsing and formatting [LocalDate] values.
 51→         *
 52→         * Example:
 53→         * ```
 54→         * // 2020 Jan 05
 55→         * LocalDate.Format {
 56→         *   year()
 57→         *   char(' ')
 58→         *   monthName(MonthNames.ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED)
 59→         *   char(' ')
 60→         *   dayOfMonth()
 61→         * }
 62→         * ```
 63→         *
 64→         * Only parsing and formatting of well-formed values is supported. If the input does not fit the boundaries
 65→         * (for example, [dayOfMonth] is 31 for February), consider using [DateTimeComponents.Format] instead.
 66→         *
 67→         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [LocalDate.Formats].
 68→         */
 69→        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 70→        public fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>
 71→
 72→        internal val MIN: LocalDate
 73→        internal val MAX: LocalDate
 74→    }
 75→
 76→    /**
 77→     * A collection of predefined formats for parsing and formatting [LocalDate] values.
 78→     *
 79→     * See [LocalDate.Formats.ISO] and [LocalDate.Formats.ISO_BASIC] for popular predefined formats.
 80→     * [LocalDate.parse] and [LocalDate.toString] can be used as convenient shortcuts for the
 81→     * [LocalDate.Formats.ISO] format.
 82→     *
 83→     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [LocalDate.Format] to create a custom
 84→     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [LocalDate] values.
 85→     */
 86→    public object Formats {
 87→        /**
 88→         * ISO 8601 extended format, which is the format used by [LocalDate.toString] and [LocalDate.parse].
 89→         *
 90→         * Examples of dates in ISO 8601 format:
 91→         * - `2020-08-30`
 92→         * - `+12020-08-30`
 93→         * - `0000-08-30`
 94→         * - `-0001-08-30`
 95→         */
 96→        public val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>
 97→
 98→        /**
 99→         * ISO 8601 basic format.
100→         *
101→         * Examples of dates in ISO 8601 basic format:
102→         * - `20200830`
103→         * - `+120200830`
104→         * - `00000830`
105→         * - `-00010830`
106→         */
107→        public val ISO_BASIC: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>
108→    }
109→
110→    /**
111→     * Constructs a [LocalDate] instance from the given date components.
112→     *
113→     * The components [monthNumber] and [dayOfMonth] are 1-based.
114→     *
115→     * The supported ranges of components:
116→     * - [year] the range is platform dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
117→     *          [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE]
118→     * - [monthNumber] `1..12`
119→     * - [dayOfMonth] `1..31`, the upper bound can be less, depending on the month
120→     *
121→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any parameter is out of range, or if [dayOfMonth] is invalid for the
122→     * given [monthNumber] and [year].
123→     */
124→    public constructor(year: Int, monthNumber: Int, dayOfMonth: Int)
125→
126→    /**
127→     * Constructs a [LocalDate] instance from the given date components.
128→     *
129→     * The supported ranges of components:
130→     * - [year] the range is platform dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
131→     *          [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE]
132→     * - [month] all values of the [Month] enum
133→     * - [dayOfMonth] `1..31`, the upper bound can be less, depending on the month
134→     *
135→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any parameter is out of range, or if [dayOfMonth] is invalid for the
136→     * given [month] and [year].
137→     */
138→    public constructor(year: Int, month: Month, dayOfMonth: Int)
139→
140→    /** Returns the year component of the date. */
141→    public val year: Int
142→
143→    /** Returns the number-of-month (1..12) component of the date. */
144→    public val monthNumber: Int
145→
146→    /** Returns the month ([Month]) component of the date. */
147→    public val month: Month
148→
149→    /** Returns the day-of-month component of the date. */
150→    public val dayOfMonth: Int
151→
152→    /** Returns the day-of-week component of the date. */

154→
155→    /** Returns the day-of-year component of the date. */

158→    /**

162→     *
163→     * @see LocalDate.fromEpochDays
164→     */
165→    public fun toEpochDays(): Int

167→    /**

169→     * Returns zero if this date represent the same day as the other (i.e. equal to other),
170→     * a negative number if this date is earlier than the other,
171→     * and a positive number if this date is later than the other.
172→     */
173→    public override fun compareTo(other: LocalDate): Int
174→
175→    /**
176→     * Converts this date to the extended ISO-8601 string representation.
177→     *
178→     * @see Formats.ISO for the format details.
179→     * @see parse for the dual operation: obtaining [LocalDate] from a string.
180→     * @see LocalDate.format for formatting using a custom format.
181→     */
182→    public override fun toString(): String
183→}

185→/**
186→ * Formats this value using the given [format].
187→ * Equivalent to calling [DateTimeFormat.format] on [format] with `this`.
188→ */
189→public fun LocalDate.format(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>): String = format.format(this)
190→
191→/**
192→ * @suppress
193→ */
194→@Deprecated("Removed to support more idiomatic code. See https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime/issues/339", ReplaceWith("LocalDate.parse(this)"), DeprecationLevel.WARNING)
195→public fun String.toLocalDate(): LocalDate = LocalDate.parse(this)

197→/**
198→ * Combines this date's components with the specified time components into a [LocalDateTime] value.
199→ *
200→ * For finding an instant that corresponds to the start of a date in a particular time zone consider using
201→ * [LocalDate.atStartOfDayIn] function because a day does not always start at the fixed time 0:00:00.
202→ */
203→public fun LocalDate.atTime(hour: Int, minute: Int, second: Int = 0, nanosecond: Int = 0): LocalDateTime =
204→    LocalDateTime(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth, hour, minute, second, nanosecond)
205→
206→/**
207→ * Combines this date's components with the specified [LocalTime] components into a [LocalDateTime] value.
208→ *
209→ * For finding an instant that corresponds to the start of a date in a particular time zone consider using
210→ * [LocalDate.atStartOfDayIn] function because a day does not always start at the fixed time 0:00:00.
211→ */
212→public fun LocalDate.atTime(time: LocalTime): LocalDateTime = LocalDateTime(this, time)

214→
215→/**
216→ * Returns a date that is the result of adding components of [DatePeriod] to this date. The components are
217→ * added in the order from the largest units to the smallest, i.e. from years to days.
218→ *
219→ * @see LocalDate.periodUntil
220→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value or the results of intermediate computations are too large to fit in
221→ * [LocalDate].
222→ */
223→public expect operator fun LocalDate.plus(period: DatePeriod): LocalDate
224→
225→/**
226→ * Returns a date that is the result of subtracting components of [DatePeriod] from this date. The components are
227→ * subtracted in the order from the largest units to the smallest, i.e. from years to days.
228→ *
229→ * @see LocalDate.periodUntil
230→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value or the results of intermediate computations are too large to fit in
231→ * [LocalDate].
232→ */
233→public operator fun LocalDate.minus(period: DatePeriod): LocalDate =
234→    if (period.days != Int.MIN_VALUE && period.months != Int.MIN_VALUE) {
235→        plus(with(period) { DatePeriod(-years, -months, -days) })
236→    } else {
237→        minus(period.years, DateTimeUnit.YEAR).minus(period.months, DateTimeUnit.MONTH)
238→            .minus(period.days, DateTimeUnit.DAY)
239→    }
240→
241→/**
242→ * Returns a [DatePeriod] representing the difference between `this` and [other] dates.
243→ *
244→ * The components of [DatePeriod] are calculated so that adding it to `this` date results in the [other] date.
245→ *
246→ * All components of the [DatePeriod] returned are:
247→ * - positive or zero if this date is earlier than the other,
248→ * - negative or zero if this date is later than the other,
249→ * - exactly zero if this date is equal to the other.
250→ *
251→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the number of months between the two dates exceeds an Int (JVM only).
252→ *
253→ * @see LocalDate.minus
254→ */
255→public expect fun LocalDate.periodUntil(other: LocalDate): DatePeriod
256→
257→/**
258→ * Returns a [DatePeriod] representing the difference between [other] and `this` dates.
259→ *
260→ * The components of [DatePeriod] are calculated so that adding it back to the `other` date results in this date.
261→ *
262→ * All components of the [DatePeriod] returned are:
263→ * - negative or zero if this date is earlier than the other,
264→ * - positive or zero if this date is later than the other,
265→ * - exactly zero if this date is equal to the other.
266→ *
267→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the number of months between the two dates exceeds an Int (JVM only).
268→ *
269→ * @see LocalDate.periodUntil
270→ */
271→public operator fun LocalDate.minus(other: LocalDate): DatePeriod = other.periodUntil(this)
272→
273→/**
274→ * Returns the whole number of the specified date [units][unit] between `this` and [other] dates.
275→ *
276→ * The value returned is:
277→ * - positive or zero if this date is earlier than the other,
278→ * - negative or zero if this date is later than the other,
279→ * - zero if this date is equal to the other.
280→
281→ * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
282→ *
283→ * @see LocalDate.daysUntil
284→ * @see LocalDate.monthsUntil
285→ * @see LocalDate.yearsUntil
286→ *
287→ */
288→public expect fun LocalDate.until(other: LocalDate, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): Int
289→
290→/**
291→ * Returns the number of whole days between two dates.
292→ *
293→ * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
294→ *
295→ * @see LocalDate.until
296→ */
297→public expect fun LocalDate.daysUntil(other: LocalDate): Int
298→
299→/**
300→ * Returns the number of whole months between two dates.
301→ *
302→ * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
303→ *
304→ * @see LocalDate.until
305→ */
306→public expect fun LocalDate.monthsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int
307→
308→/**
309→ * Returns the number of whole years between two dates.
310→ *
311→ * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
312→ *
313→ * @see LocalDate.until
314→ */
315→public expect fun LocalDate.yearsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int
316→
317→/**

319→ *
320→ * The returned date is later than this date.
321→ *
322→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the result exceeds the boundaries of [LocalDate].
323→ */
324→@Deprecated("Use the plus overload with an explicit number of units", ReplaceWith("this.plus(1, unit)"))
325→public expect fun LocalDate.plus(unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate

334→@Deprecated("Use the minus overload with an explicit number of units", ReplaceWith("this.minus(1, unit)"))

  5→@file:JvmName("LocalDateJvmKt")
  6→package kotlinx.datetime
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeAdd
 10→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeMultiply
 11→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
 12→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalDateIso8601Serializer
 13→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 14→import java.time.DateTimeException
 15→import java.time.format.DateTimeParseException
 16→import java.time.temporal.ChronoUnit
 17→import java.time.LocalDate as jtLocalDate
 18→
 19→@Serializable(with = LocalDateIso8601Serializer::class)
 20→public actual class LocalDate internal constructor(internal val value: jtLocalDate) : Comparable<LocalDate> {
 21→    public actual companion object {
 22→        public actual fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>): LocalDate =
 23→            if (format === Formats.ISO) {
 24→                try {
 25→                    jtLocalDate.parse(input).let(::LocalDate)
 26→                } catch (e: DateTimeParseException) {
 27→                    throw DateTimeFormatException(e)
 28→                }
 29→            } else {
 30→                format.parse(input)
 31→            }
 32→
 33→        @Deprecated("This overload is only kept for binary compatibility", level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN)
 34→        public fun parse(isoString: String): LocalDate = parse(input = isoString)
 35→
 36→        internal actual val MIN: LocalDate = LocalDate(jtLocalDate.MIN)
 37→        internal actual val MAX: LocalDate = LocalDate(jtLocalDate.MAX)
 38→
 39→        public actual fun fromEpochDays(epochDays: Int): LocalDate =
 40→            LocalDate(jtLocalDate.ofEpochDay(epochDays.toLong()))
 41→
 42→        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 43→        public actual fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> =
 44→            LocalDateFormat.build(block)
 45→    }
 46→
 47→    public actual object Formats {
 48→        public actual val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> get() = ISO_DATE
 49→
 50→        public actual val ISO_BASIC: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> = ISO_DATE_BASIC
 51→    }
 52→
 53→    public actual constructor(year: Int, monthNumber: Int, dayOfMonth: Int) :
 54→            this(try {
 55→                jtLocalDate.of(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth)
 56→            } catch (e: DateTimeException) {
 57→                throw IllegalArgumentException(e)
 58→            })
 59→
 60→    public actual constructor(year: Int, month: Month, dayOfMonth: Int) : this(year, month.number, dayOfMonth)
 61→
 62→    public actual val year: Int get() = value.year
 63→    public actual val monthNumber: Int get() = value.monthValue
 64→    public actual val month: Month get() = value.month
 65→    public actual val dayOfMonth: Int get() = value.dayOfMonth
 66→    public actual val dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek get() = value.dayOfWeek
 67→    public actual val dayOfYear: Int get() = value.dayOfYear
 68→
 69→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
 70→            (this === other) || (other is LocalDate && this.value == other.value)
 71→
 72→    override fun hashCode(): Int = value.hashCode()
 73→
 74→    actual override fun toString(): String = value.toString()
 75→
 76→    actual override fun compareTo(other: LocalDate): Int = this.value.compareTo(other.value)
 77→
 78→    public actual fun toEpochDays(): Int = value.toEpochDay().clampToInt()
 79→}
 80→
 81→@Deprecated("Use the plus overload with an explicit number of units", ReplaceWith("this.plus(1, unit)"))
 82→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 83→        plus(1L, unit)
 84→
 85→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 86→        plus(value.toLong(), unit)
 87→
 88→public actual fun LocalDate.minus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 89→        plus(-value.toLong(), unit)
 90→
 91→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(value: Long, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 92→        try {
 93→            when (unit) {
 94→                is DateTimeUnit.DayBased -> {
 95→                    val addDays: Long = safeMultiply(value, unit.days.toLong())
 96→                    ofEpochDayChecked(safeAdd(this.value.toEpochDay(), addDays))
 97→                }
 98→                is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased ->
 99→                    this.value.plusMonths(safeMultiply(value, unit.months.toLong()))
100→            }.let(::LocalDate)
101→        } catch (e: Exception) {
102→            if (e !is DateTimeException && e !is ArithmeticException) throw e
103→            throw DateTimeArithmeticException("The result of adding $value of $unit to $this is out of LocalDate range.", e)
104→        }
105→
106→private val minEpochDay = java.time.LocalDate.MIN.toEpochDay()
107→private val maxEpochDay = java.time.LocalDate.MAX.toEpochDay()
108→private fun ofEpochDayChecked(epochDay: Long): java.time.LocalDate {
109→    // LocalDate.ofEpochDay doesn't actually check that the argument doesn't overflow year calculation
110→    if (epochDay !in minEpochDay..maxEpochDay)
111→        throw DateTimeException("The resulting day $epochDay is out of supported LocalDate range.")
112→    return java.time.LocalDate.ofEpochDay(epochDay)
113→}
114→
115→public actual operator fun LocalDate.plus(period: DatePeriod): LocalDate = try {
116→    with(period) {
117→        return@with value
118→                .run { if (totalMonths != 0) plusMonths(totalMonths.toLong()) else this }
119→                .run { if (days != 0) plusDays(days.toLong()) else this }
120→
121→    }.let(::LocalDate)
122→} catch (e: DateTimeException) {
123→    throw DateTimeArithmeticException("The result of adding $value to $this is out of LocalDate range.")
124→}
125→
126→
127→public actual fun LocalDate.periodUntil(other: LocalDate): DatePeriod {
128→    var startD = this.value
129→    val endD = other.value
130→    val months = startD.until(endD, ChronoUnit.MONTHS); startD = startD.plusMonths(months)
131→    val days = startD.until(endD, ChronoUnit.DAYS)
132→
133→    if (months > Int.MAX_VALUE || months < Int.MIN_VALUE) {
134→        throw DateTimeArithmeticException("The number of months between $this and $other does not fit in an Int")
135→    }
136→    return DatePeriod(totalMonths = months.toInt(), days.toInt())
137→}
138→
139→public actual fun LocalDate.until(other: LocalDate, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): Int = when(unit) {
140→    is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased -> (this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.MONTHS) / unit.months).clampToInt()
141→    is DateTimeUnit.DayBased -> (this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.DAYS) / unit.days).clampToInt()
142→}
143→
144→public actual fun LocalDate.daysUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
145→        this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.DAYS).clampToInt()
146→
147→public actual fun LocalDate.monthsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
148→        this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.MONTHS).clampToInt()
149→
150→public actual fun LocalDate.yearsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
151→        this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.YEARS).clampToInt()
152→

 5→@file:JvmName("LocalTimeJvmKt")

 7→package kotlinx.datetime
 8→
 9→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
10→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
11→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalTimeIso8601Serializer
12→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
13→import java.time.DateTimeException
14→import java.time.format.DateTimeParseException
15→import java.time.LocalTime as jtLocalTime
16→
17→@Serializable(with = LocalTimeIso8601Serializer::class)
18→public actual class LocalTime internal constructor(internal val value: jtLocalTime) :
19→    Comparable<LocalTime> {
20→
21→    public actual constructor(hour: Int, minute: Int, second: Int, nanosecond: Int) :
22→            this(
23→                try {
24→                    jtLocalTime.of(hour, minute, second, nanosecond)
25→                } catch (e: DateTimeException) {
26→                    throw IllegalArgumentException(e)
27→                }
28→            )
29→
30→    public actual val hour: Int get() = value.hour
31→    public actual val minute: Int get() = value.minute
32→    public actual val second: Int get() = value.second
33→    public actual val nanosecond: Int get() = value.nano
34→    public actual fun toSecondOfDay(): Int = value.toSecondOfDay()
35→    public actual fun toMillisecondOfDay(): Int = (value.toNanoOfDay() / NANOS_PER_MILLI).toInt()
36→    public actual fun toNanosecondOfDay(): Long = value.toNanoOfDay()
37→
38→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
39→        (this === other) || (other is LocalTime && this.value == other.value)
40→
41→    override fun hashCode(): Int = value.hashCode()
42→
43→    actual override fun toString(): String = value.toString()
44→
45→    actual override fun compareTo(other: LocalTime): Int = this.value.compareTo(other.value)
46→
47→    public actual companion object {
48→        public actual fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalTime>): LocalTime =
49→            if (format === Formats.ISO) {
50→                try {
51→                    jtLocalTime.parse(input).let(::LocalTime)
52→                } catch (e: DateTimeParseException) {
53→                    throw DateTimeFormatException(e)
54→                }
55→            } else {
56→                format.parse(input)
57→            }
58→
59→        @Deprecated("This overload is only kept for binary compatibility", level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN)
60→        public fun parse(isoString: String): LocalTime = parse(input = isoString)
61→
62→        public actual fun fromSecondOfDay(secondOfDay: Int): LocalTime = try {
63→            jtLocalTime.ofSecondOfDay(secondOfDay.toLong()).let(::LocalTime)
64→        } catch (e: DateTimeException) {
65→            throw IllegalArgumentException(e)
66→        }
67→
68→        public actual fun fromMillisecondOfDay(millisecondOfDay: Int): LocalTime = try {
69→            jtLocalTime.ofNanoOfDay(millisecondOfDay * 1_000_000L).let(::LocalTime)
70→        } catch (e: Throwable) {
71→            throw IllegalArgumentException(e)
72→        }
73→
74→        public actual fun fromNanosecondOfDay(nanosecondOfDay: Long): LocalTime = try {
75→            jtLocalTime.ofNanoOfDay(nanosecondOfDay).let(::LocalTime)
76→        } catch (e: DateTimeException) {
77→            throw IllegalArgumentException(e)
78→        }
79→
80→        internal actual val MIN: LocalTime = LocalTime(jtLocalTime.MIN)
81→        internal actual val MAX: LocalTime = LocalTime(jtLocalTime.MAX)
82→
83→        @Suppress("FunctionName")
84→        public actual fun Format(builder: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalTime> =
85→            LocalTimeFormat.build(builder)
86→    }
87→
88→    public actual object Formats {
89→        public actual val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalTime> get() = ISO_TIME
90→
91→    }
92→}
93→

1→/*
  2→ * Copyright 2019-2020 JetBrains s.r.o.
  3→ * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4→ */
  5→/* Based on the ThreeTenBp project.
  6→ * Copyright (c) 2007-present, Stephen Colebourne & Michael Nascimento Santos
  7→ */
  8→
  9→package kotlinx.datetime
 10→
 11→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
 12→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
 13→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeAdd
 14→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeMultiply
 15→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalDateIso8601Serializer
 16→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 17→import kotlin.math.*
 18→
 19→internal const val YEAR_MIN = -999_999
 20→internal const val YEAR_MAX = 999_999
 21→
 22→private fun isValidYear(year: Int): Boolean =
 23→    year >= YEAR_MIN && year <= YEAR_MAX
 24→
 25→@Serializable(with = LocalDateIso8601Serializer::class)
 26→public actual class LocalDate actual constructor(public actual val year: Int, public actual val monthNumber: Int, public actual val dayOfMonth: Int) : Comparable<LocalDate> {
 27→
 28→    init {
 29→        // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#create
 30→        require(isValidYear(year)) { "Invalid date: the year is out of range" }
 31→        require(monthNumber in 1..12) { "Invalid date: month must be a number between 1 and 12, got $monthNumber" }
 32→        require(dayOfMonth in 1..31) { "Invalid date: day of month must be a number between 1 and 31, got $dayOfMonth" }
 33→        if (dayOfMonth > 28 && dayOfMonth > monthNumber.monthLength(isLeapYear(year))) {
 34→            if (dayOfMonth == 29) {
 35→                throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid date 'February 29' as '$year' is not a leap year")
 36→            } else {
 37→                throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid date '${month.name} $dayOfMonth'")
 38→            }
 39→        }
 40→    }
 41→
 42→    public actual constructor(year: Int, month: Month, dayOfMonth: Int) : this(year, month.number, dayOfMonth)
 43→
 44→    public actual companion object {
 45→        public actual fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>): LocalDate = format.parse(input)
 46→
 47→        @Deprecated("This overload is only kept for binary compatibility", level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN)
 48→        public fun parse(isoString: String): LocalDate = parse(input = isoString)
 49→
 50→        // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#toEpochDay
 51→        public actual fun fromEpochDays(epochDays: Int): LocalDate {
 52→            // LocalDate(-999999, 1, 1).toEpochDay(), LocalDate(999999, 12, 31).toEpochDay()
 53→            require(epochDays in MIN_EPOCH_DAY..MAX_EPOCH_DAY) {
 54→                "Invalid date: boundaries of LocalDate exceeded"
 55→            }
 56→            var zeroDay = epochDays + DAYS_0000_TO_1970
 57→            // find the march-based year
 58→            zeroDay -= 60 // adjust to 0000-03-01 so leap day is at end of four year cycle
 59→
 60→            var adjust = 0
 61→            if (zeroDay < 0) { // adjust negative years to positive for calculation
 62→                val adjustCycles = (zeroDay + 1) / DAYS_PER_CYCLE - 1
 63→                adjust = adjustCycles * 400
 64→                zeroDay += -adjustCycles * DAYS_PER_CYCLE
 65→            }
 66→            var yearEst = ((400 * zeroDay.toLong() + 591) / DAYS_PER_CYCLE).toInt()
 67→            var doyEst = zeroDay - (365 * yearEst + yearEst / 4 - yearEst / 100 + yearEst / 400)
 68→            if (doyEst < 0) { // fix estimate
 69→                yearEst--
 70→                doyEst = zeroDay - (365 * yearEst + yearEst / 4 - yearEst / 100 + yearEst / 400)
 71→            }
 72→            yearEst += adjust // reset any negative year
 73→
 74→            val marchDoy0 = doyEst
 75→
 76→            // convert march-based values back to january-based
 77→            val marchMonth0 = (marchDoy0 * 5 + 2) / 153
 78→            val month = (marchMonth0 + 2) % 12 + 1
 79→            val dom = marchDoy0 - (marchMonth0 * 306 + 5) / 10 + 1
 80→            yearEst += marchMonth0 / 10
 81→
 82→            return LocalDate(yearEst, month, dom)
 83→        }
 84→
 85→        internal actual val MIN = LocalDate(YEAR_MIN, 1, 1)
 86→        internal actual val MAX = LocalDate(YEAR_MAX, 12, 31)
 87→
 88→        internal const val MIN_EPOCH_DAY = -365961662
 89→        internal const val MAX_EPOCH_DAY = 364522971
 90→
 91→        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 92→        public actual fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> =
 93→            LocalDateFormat.build(block)
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    public actual object Formats {
 97→        public actual val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> get() = ISO_DATE
 98→
 99→        public actual val ISO_BASIC: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> = ISO_DATE_BASIC
100→    }
101→
102→    // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#toEpochDay
103→    public actual fun toEpochDays(): Int {
104→        val y = year
105→        val m = monthNumber
106→        var total = 0
107→        total += 365 * y
108→        if (y >= 0) {
109→            total += (y + 3) / 4 - (y + 99) / 100 + (y + 399) / 400
110→        } else {
111→            total -= y / -4 - y / -100 + y / -400
112→        }
113→        total += ((367 * m - 362) / 12)
114→        total += dayOfMonth - 1
115→        if (m > 2) {
116→            total--
117→            if (!isLeapYear(year)) {
118→                total--
119→            }
120→        }
121→        return total - DAYS_0000_TO_1970
122→    }
123→
124→    public actual val month: Month
125→        get() = Month(monthNumber)
126→
127→    // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#getDayOfWeek
128→    public actual val dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek
129→        get() {
130→            val dow0 = (toEpochDays() + 3).mod(7)
131→            return DayOfWeek(dow0 + 1)
132→        }
133→
134→    // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#getDayOfYear
135→    public actual val dayOfYear: Int
136→        get() = month.firstDayOfYear(isLeapYear(year)) + dayOfMonth - 1
137→
138→    // Several times faster than using `compareBy`
139→    actual override fun compareTo(other: LocalDate): Int {
140→        val y = year.compareTo(other.year)
141→        if (y != 0) {
142→            return y
143→        }
144→        val m = monthNumber.compareTo(other.monthNumber)
145→        if (m != 0) {
146→            return m
147→        }
148→        return dayOfMonth.compareTo(other.dayOfMonth)
149→    }
150→
151→    // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#resolvePreviousValid
152→    /**
153→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the result exceeds the boundaries
154→     */
155→    private fun resolvePreviousValid(year: Int, month: Int, day: Int): LocalDate {
156→        val newDay = min(day, month.monthLength(isLeapYear(year)))
157→        return LocalDate(year, month, newDay)
158→    }
159→
160→    // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#plusMonths
161→    /**
162→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the result exceeds the boundaries
163→     * @throws ArithmeticException if arithmetic overflow occurs
164→     */
165→    internal fun plusMonths(monthsToAdd: Int): LocalDate {
166→        if (monthsToAdd == 0) {
167→            return this
168→        }
169→        val monthCount = year * 12 + (monthNumber - 1)
170→        val calcMonths = safeAdd(monthCount, monthsToAdd)
171→        val newYear = calcMonths.floorDiv(12)
172→        val newMonth = calcMonths.mod(12) + 1
173→        return resolvePreviousValid(newYear, newMonth, dayOfMonth)
174→    }
175→
176→    // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#plusDays
177→    /**
178→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the result exceeds the boundaries
179→     * @throws ArithmeticException if arithmetic overflow occurs
180→     */
181→    internal fun plusDays(daysToAdd: Int): LocalDate =
182→        if (daysToAdd == 0) this
183→        else fromEpochDays(safeAdd(toEpochDays(), daysToAdd))
184→
185→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
186→        this === other || (other is LocalDate && compareTo(other) == 0)
187→
188→    // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#hashCode
189→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
190→        val yearValue = year
191→        val monthValue: Int = monthNumber
192→        val dayValue: Int = dayOfMonth
193→        return yearValue and -0x800 xor (yearValue shl 11) + (monthValue shl 6) + dayValue
194→    }
195→
196→    // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#toString
197→    actual override fun toString(): String = format(Formats.ISO)
198→}
199→
200→@Deprecated("Use the plus overload with an explicit number of units", ReplaceWith("this.plus(1, unit)"))
201→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
202→        plus(1, unit)
203→
204→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
205→    try {
206→        when (unit) {
207→            is DateTimeUnit.DayBased -> plusDays(safeMultiply(value, unit.days))
208→            is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased -> plusMonths(safeMultiply(value, unit.months))
209→        }
210→    } catch (e: ArithmeticException) {
211→        throw DateTimeArithmeticException("Arithmetic overflow when adding a value to a date", e)
212→    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
213→        throw DateTimeArithmeticException("Boundaries of LocalDate exceeded when adding a value", e)
214→    }
215→
216→public actual fun LocalDate.minus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate = plus(-value, unit)
217→
218→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(value: Long, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
219→        if (value > Int.MAX_VALUE || value < Int.MIN_VALUE)
220→            throw DateTimeArithmeticException("Can't add a Long to a LocalDate") // TODO: less specific message
221→        else plus(value.toInt(), unit)
222→
223→public actual operator fun LocalDate.plus(period: DatePeriod): LocalDate =
224→    with(period) {
225→        try {
226→            this@plus
227→                .run { if (totalMonths != 0) plusMonths(totalMonths) else this }
228→                .run { if (days != 0) plusDays(days) else this }
229→        } catch (e: ArithmeticException) {
230→            throw DateTimeArithmeticException("Arithmetic overflow when adding a period to a date", e)
231→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
232→            throw DateTimeArithmeticException("Boundaries of LocalDate exceeded when adding a period", e)
233→        }
234→    }
235→
236→public actual fun LocalDate.until(other: LocalDate, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): Int = when(unit) {
237→    is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased -> monthsUntil(other) / unit.months
238→    is DateTimeUnit.DayBased -> daysUntil(other) / unit.days
239→}
240→
241→// org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#daysUntil
242→public actual fun LocalDate.daysUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
243→    other.toEpochDays() - this.toEpochDays()
244→
245→// org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#getProlepticMonth
246→internal val LocalDate.prolepticMonth get() = (year * 12) + (monthNumber - 1)
247→
248→// org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#monthsUntil
249→public actual fun LocalDate.monthsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int {
250→    val packed1 = prolepticMonth * 32 + dayOfMonth
251→    val packed2 = other.prolepticMonth * 32 + other.dayOfMonth
252→    return (packed2 - packed1) / 32
253→}
254→
255→public actual fun LocalDate.yearsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
256→    monthsUntil(other) / 12
257→
258→public actual fun LocalDate.periodUntil(other: LocalDate): DatePeriod {
259→    val months = monthsUntil(other)
260→    val days = plusMonths(months).daysUntil(other)
261→    return DatePeriod(totalMonths = months, days)
262→}
263→
264→internal fun LocalDate.previousOrSame(dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek) =
265→    minus((this.dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber - dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber).mod(7), DateTimeUnit.DAY)
266→
267→internal fun LocalDate.nextOrSame(dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek) =
268→    plus((dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber - this.dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber).mod(7), DateTimeUnit.DAY)
269→

1→/*
  2→ * Copyright 2019-2020 JetBrains s.r.o.
  3→ * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4→ */
  5→/* Based on the ThreeTenBp project.
  6→ * Copyright (c) 2007-present, Stephen Colebourne & Michael Nascimento Santos
  7→ */
  8→
  9→package kotlinx.datetime
 10→
 11→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
 12→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
 13→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalTimeIso8601Serializer
 14→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 15→
 16→@Serializable(LocalTimeIso8601Serializer::class)
 17→public actual class LocalTime actual constructor(
 18→    public actual val hour: Int,
 19→    public actual val minute: Int,
 20→    public actual val second: Int,
 21→    public actual val nanosecond: Int
 22→    ) : Comparable<LocalTime> {
 23→
 24→    init {
 25→        fun check(value: Int, lower: Int, upper: Int, str: String) =
 26→            require(value in lower..upper) {
 27→                "Invalid time: $str must be a number between $lower and $upper, got $value"
 28→            }
 29→        check(hour, 0, 23, "hour")
 30→        check(minute, 0, 59, "minute")
 31→        check(second, 0, 59, "second")
 32→        check(nanosecond, 0, NANOS_PER_ONE - 1, "nanosecond")
 33→    }
 34→
 35→    public actual companion object {
 36→        public actual fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalTime>): LocalTime = format.parse(input)
 37→
 38→        @Deprecated("This overload is only kept for binary compatibility", level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN)
 39→        public fun parse(isoString: String): LocalTime = parse(input = isoString)
 40→
 41→        public actual fun fromSecondOfDay(secondOfDay: Int): LocalTime =
 42→            ofSecondOfDay(secondOfDay, 0)
 43→
 44→        public actual fun fromMillisecondOfDay(millisecondOfDay: Int): LocalTime =
 45→            ofNanoOfDay(millisecondOfDay.toLong() * NANOS_PER_MILLI)
 46→
 47→        public actual fun fromNanosecondOfDay(nanosecondOfDay: Long): LocalTime =
 48→            ofNanoOfDay(nanosecondOfDay)
 49→
 50→        // org.threeten.bp.LocalTime#ofSecondOfDay(long, int)
 51→        internal fun ofSecondOfDay(secondOfDay: Int, nanoOfSecond: Int): LocalTime {
 52→            require(secondOfDay in 0 until SECONDS_PER_DAY)
 53→            require(nanoOfSecond in 0 until NANOS_PER_ONE)
 54→            val hours = (secondOfDay / SECONDS_PER_HOUR)
 55→            val secondWithoutHours = secondOfDay - hours * SECONDS_PER_HOUR
 56→            val minutes = (secondWithoutHours / SECONDS_PER_MINUTE)
 57→            val second = secondWithoutHours - minutes * SECONDS_PER_MINUTE
 58→            return LocalTime(hours, minutes, second, nanoOfSecond)
 59→        }
 60→
 61→        internal fun of(hour: Int, minute: Int, second: Int, nanosecond: Int): LocalTime {
 62→            return LocalTime(hour, minute, second, nanosecond)
 63→        }
 64→
 65→        // org.threeten.bp.LocalTime#ofNanoOfDay
 66→        internal fun ofNanoOfDay(nanoOfDay: Long): LocalTime {
 67→            require(nanoOfDay >= 0 && nanoOfDay < SECONDS_PER_DAY.toLong() * NANOS_PER_ONE)
 68→            var newNanoOfDay = nanoOfDay
 69→            val hours = (newNanoOfDay / NANOS_PER_HOUR).toInt()
 70→            newNanoOfDay -= hours * NANOS_PER_HOUR
 71→            val minutes = (newNanoOfDay / NANOS_PER_MINUTE).toInt()
 72→            newNanoOfDay -= minutes * NANOS_PER_MINUTE
 73→            val seconds = (newNanoOfDay / NANOS_PER_ONE).toInt()
 74→            newNanoOfDay -= seconds * NANOS_PER_ONE
 75→            return LocalTime(hours, minutes, seconds, newNanoOfDay.toInt())
 76→        }
 77→
 78→        internal actual val MIN: LocalTime = LocalTime(0, 0, 0, 0)
 79→        internal actual val MAX: LocalTime = LocalTime(23, 59, 59, NANOS_PER_ONE - 1)
 80→
 81→        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 82→        public actual fun Format(builder: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalTime> =
 83→            LocalTimeFormat.build(builder)
 84→    }
 85→
 86→    public actual object Formats {
 87→        public actual val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalTime> get() = ISO_TIME
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    // Several times faster than using `compareBy`
 91→    actual override fun compareTo(other: LocalTime): Int {
 92→        val h = hour.compareTo(other.hour)
 93→        if (h != 0) {
 94→            return h
 95→        }
 96→        val m = minute.compareTo(other.minute)
 97→        if (m != 0) {
 98→            return m
 99→        }
100→        val s = second.compareTo(other.second)
101→        if (s != 0) {
102→            return s
103→        }
104→        return nanosecond.compareTo(other.nanosecond)
105→    }
106→
107→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
108→        val nod: Long = this.toNanosecondOfDay()
109→        return (nod xor (nod ushr 32)).toInt()
110→    }
111→
112→    // org.threeten.bp.LocalTime#toSecondOfDay
113→    public actual fun toSecondOfDay(): Int {
114→        var total: Int = hour * SECONDS_PER_HOUR
115→        total += minute * SECONDS_PER_MINUTE
116→        total += second
117→        return total
118→    }
119→
120→    public actual fun toMillisecondOfDay(): Int =
121→        toSecondOfDay() * MILLIS_PER_ONE + nanosecond / NANOS_PER_MILLI
122→
123→    // org.threeten.bp.LocalTime#toNanoOfDay
124→    public actual fun toNanosecondOfDay(): Long {
125→        var total: Long = hour.toLong() * NANOS_PER_ONE * SECONDS_PER_HOUR
126→        total += minute.toLong() * NANOS_PER_ONE * SECONDS_PER_MINUTE
127→        total += second.toLong() * NANOS_PER_ONE
128→        total += nanosecond.toLong()
129→        return total
130→    }
131→
132→    actual override fun toString(): String = format(ISO_TIME_OPTIONAL_SECONDS_TRAILING_ZEROS)
133→
134→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
135→        other is LocalTime && this.compareTo(other) == 0
136→
137→}
138→
139→internal val ISO_TIME_OPTIONAL_SECONDS_TRAILING_ZEROS by lazy {
140→    LocalTimeFormat.build {
141→        hour()
142→        char(':')
143→        minute()
144→        optional {
145→            char(':')
146→            second()
147→            optional {
148→                char('.')
149→                secondFractionInternal(1, 9, FractionalSecondDirective.GROUP_BY_THREE)
150→            }
151→        }
152→    }
153→}
154→

  5→
  6→package kotlinx.datetime
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.UtcOffsetSerializer
 10→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 11→
 12→/**
 13→ * An offset from UTC.
 14→ *
 15→ * Examples of these values:
 16→ * - `Z`, an offset of zero;
 17→ * - `+05`, plus five hours;
 18→ * - `-02`, minus two hours;
 19→ * - `+03:30`, plus three hours and thirty minutes;
 20→ * - `+01:23:45`, plus one hour, 23 minutes, and 45 seconds.
 21→ */
 22→@Serializable(with = UtcOffsetSerializer::class)
 23→public expect class UtcOffset {
 24→    /**
 25→     * The number of seconds from UTC.
 26→     *
 27→     * The larger the value, the earlier some specific civil date/time happens with the offset.
 28→     */
 29→    public val totalSeconds: Int
 30→
 31→    // TODO: Declare and document toString/equals/hashCode
 32→
 33→    public companion object {
 34→        /**
 35→         * The zero offset from UTC, `Z`.
 36→         */
 37→        public val ZERO: UtcOffset
 38→
 39→        /**
 40→         * A shortcut for calling [DateTimeFormat.parse].
 41→         *
 42→         * Parses a string that represents a UTC offset and returns the parsed [UtcOffset] value.
 43→         *
 44→         * If [format] is not specified, [Formats.ISO] is used.
 45→         *
 46→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [UtcOffset] are
 47→         * exceeded.
 48→         */
 49→        public fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset> = getIsoUtcOffestFormat()): UtcOffset
 50→
 51→        /**
 52→         * Creates a new format for parsing and formatting [UtcOffset] values.
 53→         *
 54→         * Example:
 55→         * ```
 56→         * // `GMT` on zero, `+4:30:15`, using a custom format:
 57→         * UtcOffset.Format {
 58→         *   optional("GMT") {
 59→         *     offsetHours(Padding.NONE); char(':'); offsetMinutesOfHour()
 60→         *     optional { char(':'); offsetSecondsOfMinute() }
 61→         *   }
 62→         * }
 63→         * ```
 64→         *
 65→         * Since [UtcOffset] values are rarely formatted and parsed on their own,
 66→         * instances of [DateTimeFormat] obtained here will likely need to be passed to
 67→         * [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset.offset] in a format builder for a larger data structure.
 68→         *
 69→         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [UtcOffset.Formats].
 70→         */
 71→        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 72→        public fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    /**
 76→     * A collection of predefined formats for parsing and formatting [UtcOffset] values.
 77→     *
 78→     * See [UtcOffset.Formats.ISO], [UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC], and [UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS]
 79→     * for popular predefined formats.
 80→     *
 81→     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [UtcOffset.Format] to create a custom
 82→     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [UtcOffset] values.
 83→     */
 84→    public object Formats {
 85→        /**
 86→         * ISO 8601 extended format, which is the format used by [UtcOffset.parse] and [UtcOffset.toString].
 87→         *
 88→         * An extension of the ISO 8601 is that this format allows parsing and formatting seconds.
 89→         *
 90→         * When formatting, seconds are omitted if they are zero. If the whole offset is zero, the letter `Z` is output.
 91→         *
 92→         * Examples of UTC offsets in ISO 8601 format:
 93→         * - `Z` or `+00:00`, an offset of zero;
 94→         * - `+05:00`, five hours;
 95→         * - `-02:00`, minus two hours;
 96→         * - `-17:16`
 97→         * - `+10:36:30`
 98→         */
 99→        public val ISO: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>
100→
101→        /**
102→         * ISO 8601 basic format.
103→         *
104→         * An extension of the ISO 8601 is that this format allows parsing and formatting seconds.
105→         *
106→         * When formatting, seconds are omitted if they are zero. If the whole offset is zero, the letter `Z` is output.
107→         *
108→         * Examples of UTC offsets in ISO 8601 basic format:
109→         * - `Z`
110→         * - `+05`
111→         * - `-1716`
112→         * - `+103630`
113→         *
114→         * @see UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS
115→         */
116→        public val ISO_BASIC: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>
117→
118→        /**
119→         * A subset of the ISO 8601 basic format that always outputs and parses exactly a numeric sign and four digits:
120→         * two digits for the hour and two digits for the minute. If the offset has a non-zero number of seconds,
121→         * they are truncated.
122→         *
123→         * Examples of UTC offsets in this format:
124→         * - `+0000`
125→         * - `+0500`
126→         * - `-1716`
127→         * - `+1036`
128→         *
129→         * @see UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC
130→         */
131→        public val FOUR_DIGITS: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>
132→    }
133→
134→    /**
135→     * Converts this UTC offset to the extended ISO-8601 string representation.
136→     *
137→     * @see Formats.ISO for the format details.
138→     * @see parse for the dual operation: obtaining [UtcOffset] from a string.
139→     * @see UtcOffset.format for formatting using a custom format.
140→     */
141→    public override fun toString(): String
142→}
143→
144→/**
145→ * Formats this value using the given [format].
146→ * Equivalent to calling [DateTimeFormat.format] on [format] with `this`.
147→ */
148→public fun UtcOffset.format(format: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>): String = format.format(this)
149→
150→/**
151→ * Constructs a [UtcOffset] from hours, minutes, and seconds components.
152→ *
153→ * All components must have the same sign.
154→ *
155→ * The bounds are checked: it is invalid to pass something other than `±[0; 59]` as the number of seconds or minutes.
156→ * For example, `UtcOffset(hours = 3, minutes = 61)` is invalid.
157→ *
158→ * However, the non-null component of the highest order can exceed these bounds,
159→ * for example, `UtcOffset(minutes = 241)` is valid.
160→ *
161→ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if a component exceeds its bounds when a higher order component is specified.
162→ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if components have different signs.
163→ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the resulting `UtcOffset` value is outside of range `±18:00`.
164→ */
165→public expect fun UtcOffset(hours: Int? = null, minutes: Int? = null, seconds: Int? = null): UtcOffset
166→
167→@Deprecated("Use UtcOffset.ZERO instead", ReplaceWith("UtcOffset.ZERO"), DeprecationLevel.ERROR)
168→public fun UtcOffset(): UtcOffset = UtcOffset.ZERO
169→
170→/**
171→ * Returns the fixed-offset time zone with the given UTC offset.
172→ */
173→public fun UtcOffset.asTimeZone(): FixedOffsetTimeZone = FixedOffsetTimeZone(this)
174→
175→// workaround for https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-65484
176→internal fun getIsoUtcOffestFormat() = UtcOffset.Formats.ISO
177→

1→/*
  2→ * Copyright 2019-2023 JetBrains s.r.o. and contributors.
  3→ * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4→ */
  5→
  6→package kotlinx.datetime.format
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.*
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.*
 10→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.*
 11→
 12→/**
 13→ * A format for parsing and formatting date-time-related values.
 14→ */
 15→public sealed interface DateTimeFormat<T> {
 16→    /**
 17→     * Formats the given [value] into a string, using this format.
 18→     */
 19→    public fun format(value: T): String
 20→
 21→    /**
 22→     * Formats the given [value] into the given [appendable] using this format.
 23→     */
 24→    public fun <A : Appendable> formatTo(appendable: A, value: T): A
 25→
 26→    /**
 27→     * Parses the given [input] string as [T] using this format.
 28→     *
 29→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the input string is not in the expected format or the value is invalid.
 30→     */
 31→    public fun parse(input: CharSequence): T
 32→
 33→    /**
 34→     * Parses the given [input] string as [T] using this format.
 35→     *
 36→     * @return the parsed value, or `null` if the input string is not in the expected format or the value is invalid.
 37→     */
 38→    public fun parseOrNull(input: CharSequence): T?
 39→
 40→    public companion object {
 41→        /**
 42→         * Produces Kotlin code that, when pasted into a Kotlin source file, creates a [DateTimeFormat] instance that
 43→         * behaves identically to [format].
 44→         *
 45→         * The typical use case for this is to create a [DateTimeFormat] instance using a non-idiomatic approach and
 46→         * then convert it to a builder DSL.
 47→         */
 48→        public fun formatAsKotlinBuilderDsl(format: DateTimeFormat<*>): String = when (format) {
 49→            is AbstractDateTimeFormat<*, *> -> format.actualFormat.builderString(allFormatConstants)
 50→        }
 51→    }
 52→}
 53→
 54→/**
 55→ * The style of padding to use when formatting a value.
 56→ */
 57→public enum class Padding {
 58→    /**
 59→     * No padding.
 60→     */
 61→    NONE,
 62→
 63→    /**
 64→     * Pad with zeros.
 65→     */
 66→    ZERO,
 67→
 68→    /**
 69→     * Pad with spaces.
 70→     */
 71→    SPACE
 72→}
 73→
 74→internal fun Padding.toKotlinCode(): String = when (this) {
 75→    Padding.NONE -> "Padding.NONE"
 76→    Padding.ZERO -> "Padding.ZERO"
 77→    Padding.SPACE -> "Padding.SPACE"
 78→}
 79→
 80→internal inline fun Padding.minDigits(width: Int) = if (this == Padding.ZERO) width else 1
 81→internal inline fun Padding.spaces(width: Int) = if (this == Padding.SPACE) width else null
 82→
 83→/** [T] is the user-visible type, whereas [U] is its mutable representation for parsing and formatting. */
 84→internal sealed class AbstractDateTimeFormat<T, U : Copyable<U>> : DateTimeFormat<T> {
 85→
 86→    abstract val actualFormat: CachedFormatStructure<U>
 87→
 88→    abstract fun intermediateFromValue(value: T): U
 89→
 90→    abstract fun valueFromIntermediate(intermediate: U): T
 91→
 92→    abstract val emptyIntermediate: U // should be part of the `Copyable` interface once the language allows this
 93→
 94→    open fun valueFromIntermediateOrNull(intermediate: U): T? = try {
 95→        valueFromIntermediate(intermediate)
 96→    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 97→        null
 98→    }
 99→
100→    override fun format(value: T): String = StringBuilder().also {
101→        actualFormat.formatter().format(intermediateFromValue(value), it)
102→    }.toString()
103→
104→    override fun <A : Appendable> formatTo(appendable: A, value: T): A = appendable.apply {
105→        actualFormat.formatter().format(intermediateFromValue(value), this)
106→    }
107→
108→    override fun parse(input: CharSequence): T {
109→        val matched = try {
110→            // without the fully qualified name, the compilation fails for some reason
111→            Parser(actualFormat.parser()).match(input, emptyIntermediate)
112→        } catch (e: ParseException) {
113→            throw DateTimeFormatException("Failed to parse value from '$input'", e)
114→        }
115→        try {
116→            return valueFromIntermediate(matched)
117→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
118→            throw DateTimeFormatException(e.message!!)
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    override fun parseOrNull(input: CharSequence): T? =
123→        // without the fully qualified name, the compilation fails for some reason
124→        Parser(actualFormat.parser()).matchOrNull(input, emptyIntermediate)?.let { valueFromIntermediateOrNull(it) }
125→
126→}
127→
128→private val allFormatConstants: List<Pair<String, CachedFormatStructure<*>>> by lazy {
129→    fun unwrap(format: DateTimeFormat<*>): CachedFormatStructure<*> = (format as AbstractDateTimeFormat<*, *>).actualFormat
130→    // the formats are ordered vaguely by decreasing length, as the topmost among suitable ones is chosen.
131→    listOf(
132→        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents::dateTimeComponents.name}(DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123)" to
133→            unwrap(DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123),
134→        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents::dateTimeComponents.name}(DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET)" to
135→            unwrap(DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET),
136→        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTime::date.name}(LocalDateTime.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(LocalDateTime.Formats.ISO),
137→        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::date.name}(LocalDate.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(LocalDate.Formats.ISO),
138→        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::date.name}(LocalDate.Formats.ISO_BASIC)" to unwrap(LocalDate.Formats.ISO_BASIC),
139→        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::time.name}(LocalTime.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(LocalTime.Formats.ISO),
140→        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset::offset.name}(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO),
141→        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset::offset.name}(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC)" to unwrap(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC),
142→        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset::offset.name}(UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS)" to unwrap(UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS),
143→    )
144→}
145→

1→/*

  7→

 10→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.*
 11→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.Copyable
 12→
 13→/**
 14→ * A description of how month names are formatted.
 15→ */
 16→public class MonthNames(
 17→    /**
 18→     * A list of month names, in order from January to December.
 19→     */
 20→    public val names: List<String>
 21→) {
 22→    init {
 23→        require(names.size == 12) { "Month names must contain exactly 12 elements" }
 24→    }
 25→
 26→    /**
 27→     * Create a [MonthNames] using the month names in order from January to December.
 28→     */
 29→    public constructor(
 30→        january: String, february: String, march: String, april: String, may: String, june: String,

1→/*

  6→package kotlinx.datetime.internal.format
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.DecimalFraction
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.formatter.*
 10→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.*
 11→
 12→/**
 13→ * A directive that specifies a way to parse and format the [field].
 14→ */
 15→internal interface FieldFormatDirective<in Target> {
 16→    /**
 17→     * The field parsed and formatted by this directive.
 18→     */
 19→    val field: FieldSpec<Target, *>
 20→
 21→    /**
 22→     * The formatter operation that formats the field.
 23→     */
 24→    fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target>
 25→
 26→    /**
 27→     * The parser structure that parses the field.
 28→     */
 29→    fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target>
 30→
 31→    /**
 32→     * The string with the code that, when evaluated in the builder context, appends the directive.
 33→     */
 34→    val builderRepresentation: String
 35→}
 36→
 37→/**
 38→ * A directive for a decimal format of an integer field that is known to be unsigned.
 39→ * The field is formatted with the field padded to [minDigits] with zeroes,
 40→ * and the parser expects the field to be at least [minDigits] digits long.
 41→ */
 42→internal abstract class UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
 43→    final override val field: UnsignedFieldSpec<Target>,
 44→    private val minDigits: Int,
 45→    private val spacePadding: Int?,
 46→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
 47→
 48→    private val maxDigits: Int = field.maxDigits
 49→
 50→    init {
 51→        require(minDigits >= 0) {
 52→            "The minimum number of digits ($minDigits) is negative"
 53→        }
 54→        require(maxDigits >= minDigits) {
 55→            "The maximum number of digits ($maxDigits) is less than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
 56→        }
 57→        if (spacePadding != null) {
 58→            require(spacePadding > minDigits) {
 59→                "The space padding ($spacePadding) should be more than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
 60→            }
 61→        }
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> {
 65→        val formatter = UnsignedIntFormatterStructure(
 66→            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
 67→            zeroPadding = minDigits,
 68→        )
 69→        return if (spacePadding != null) SpacePaddedFormatter(formatter, spacePadding) else formatter
 70→    }
 71→
 72→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
 73→        spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, maxDigits, spacePadding, field.accessor, field.name)
 74→}
 75→
 76→/**
 77→ * A directive for a string-based format of an integer field that is known to be unsigned.
 78→ */
 79→internal abstract class NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
 80→    final override val field: UnsignedFieldSpec<Target>,
 81→    private val values: List<String>,
 82→    private val name: String,
 83→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
 84→
 85→    init {
 86→        require(values.size == field.maxValue - field.minValue + 1) {
 87→            "The number of values (${values.size}) in $values does not match the range of the field (${field.maxValue - field.minValue + 1})"
 88→        }
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    private fun getStringValue(target: Target): String = field.accessor.getterNotNull(target).let {
 92→        values.getOrNull(it-field.minValue)
 93→            ?: "The value $it of ${field.name} does not have a corresponding string representation"
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    private inner class AssignableString: AssignableField<Target, String> {
 97→        override fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Target, newValue: String): String? =
 98→            field.accessor.trySetWithoutReassigning(container, values.indexOf(newValue) + field.minValue)?.let {
 99→                values[it - field.minValue]
100→            }
101→
102→        override val name: String get() = this@NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective.name
103→    }
104→
105→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
106→        StringFormatterStructure(::getStringValue)
107→
108→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
109→        ParserStructure(
110→            listOf(
111→                StringSetParserOperation(values, AssignableString(), "One of $values for $name")
112→            ), emptyList()
113→        )
114→}
115→
116→/**
117→ * A directive for a string-based format of an enum field.
118→ */
119→internal abstract class NamedEnumIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target, Type>(
120→    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Type>,
121→    private val mapping: Map<Type, String>,
122→    private val name: String,
123→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
124→
125→    private val reverseMapping = mapping.entries.associate { it.value to it.key }
126→
127→    private fun getStringValue(target: Target): String = field.accessor.getterNotNull(target).let {
128→        mapping[field.accessor.getterNotNull(target)]
129→            ?: "The value $it of ${field.name} does not have a corresponding string representation"
130→    }
131→
132→    private inner class AssignableString: AssignableField<Target, String> {
133→        override fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Target, newValue: String): String? =
134→            field.accessor.trySetWithoutReassigning(container, reverseMapping[newValue]!!)?.let { mapping[it] }
135→
136→        override val name: String get() = this@NamedEnumIntFieldFormatDirective.name
137→    }
138→
139→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
140→        StringFormatterStructure(::getStringValue)
141→
142→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
143→        ParserStructure(
144→            listOf(
145→                StringSetParserOperation(mapping.values, AssignableString(), "One of ${mapping.values} for $name")
146→            ), emptyList()
147→        )
148→}
149→
150→internal abstract class StringFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
151→    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, String>,
152→    private val acceptedStrings: Set<String>,
153→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
154→
155→    init {
156→        require(acceptedStrings.isNotEmpty()) {
157→            "The set of accepted strings is empty"
158→        }
159→    }
160→
161→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
162→        StringFormatterStructure(field.accessor::getterNotNull)
163→
164→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
165→        ParserStructure(
166→            listOf(StringSetParserOperation(acceptedStrings, field.accessor, field.name)),
167→            emptyList()
168→        )
169→}
170→
171→internal abstract class SignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
172→    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Int>,
173→    private val minDigits: Int?,
174→    private val maxDigits: Int?,
175→    private val spacePadding: Int?,
176→    private val outputPlusOnExceededWidth: Int?,
177→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
178→
179→    init {
180→        require(minDigits == null || minDigits >= 0) { "The minimum number of digits ($minDigits) is negative" }
181→        require(maxDigits == null || minDigits == null || maxDigits >= minDigits) {
182→            "The maximum number of digits ($maxDigits) is less than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
183→        }
184→    }
185→
186→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> {
187→        val formatter = SignedIntFormatterStructure(
188→            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
189→            zeroPadding = minDigits ?: 0,
190→            outputPlusOnExceededWidth = outputPlusOnExceededWidth,
191→        )
192→        return if (spacePadding != null) SpacePaddedFormatter(formatter, spacePadding) else formatter
193→    }
194→
195→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
196→        SignedIntParser(
197→            minDigits = minDigits,
198→            maxDigits = maxDigits,
199→            spacePadding = spacePadding,
200→            field.accessor,
201→            field.name,
202→            plusOnExceedsWidth = outputPlusOnExceededWidth,
203→        )
204→}
205→
206→internal abstract class DecimalFractionFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
207→    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, DecimalFraction>,
208→    private val minDigits: Int,
209→    private val maxDigits: Int,
210→    private val zerosToAdd: List<Int>,
211→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
212→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
213→        DecimalFractionFormatterStructure(field.accessor::getterNotNull, minDigits, maxDigits, zerosToAdd)
214→
215→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> = ParserStructure(
216→        listOf(
217→            NumberSpanParserOperation(
218→                listOf(FractionPartConsumer(minDigits, maxDigits, field.accessor, field.name))
219→            )
220→        ),
221→        emptyList()
222→    )
223→}
224→
225→internal abstract class ReducedIntFieldDirective<in Target>(
226→    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Int>,
227→    private val digits: Int,
228→    private val base: Int,
229→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
230→
231→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
232→        ReducedIntFormatterStructure(
233→            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
234→            digits = digits,
235→            base = base,
236→        )
237→
238→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
239→        ReducedIntParser(digits = digits, base = base, field.accessor, field.name)
240→}
